channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bin spilling out onto pavement, 21st June
item.description:
This bin has been there for at least a week now. It's a plastics recycling bin but has been filled with unsuitable rubbish so hasn't been collected. As it's been left on the street it is now being used for all kinds of rubbish from passers-by and is now overflowing and littering the pavement including a clear rubbish bag and bottles. Please can it be collected as part of the general waste so that it can be moved back into the property rather than being left on the pavement. Thank you for your help.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 126, Oakfield Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Religious propaganda, 21st June
item.description:
Unofficial and inappropriate religious poster at end of Cazanove and Upper Clapton Road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Upper Clapton Road, London

item.title: Large overhanging tree, 21st June
item.description:
Hi, the tree at the back of our property in Longlands recreation park has grown massively over the last few years and is now overhanging substantially into our garden. It is dropping a lot of seeds and leaves into our garden from these overhanging branches (see photos attached) please can we request that this is cut back to the stop the overhang.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Appledore Crescent, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Significant grafitt attack, 21st June
item.description:
Significant grafitti attack on two buildings plus a foreshore lock gate. Earlier grafitt not being cleaned has attracted further attack in the same area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Highbridge Wharf, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Scumbags, 21st June

Please add this removed ASAP before it becomes a magnet for other scumbags

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cambourne Avenue, Edmonton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees outside numbers 20 - 34 Moorfield Road, 21st June
item.description:
Street trees are causing ongoing issues that I would like the Council to inspect as a group rather than individually. During the aphid season, the trees produce extremely heavy honeydew. Vehicles parked along this side of the road become coated in a sticky residue within hours of being cleaned. This is a persistent and widespread issue affecting multiple properties along the street. In addition, many of the trees have substantial basal growth around their trunks. In several cases this growth significantly narrows the usable width of the pavement. Pedestrians are frequently forced to step into the carriageway to pass, particularly where two people are walking together or where pushchairs are being used. This creates a potential safety concern due to the proximity of moving traffic. This section of road is directly opposite a nursery, meaning the pavement is heavily used by parents, young children, and pushchairs during peak drop-off and collection times, when both pedestrian and vehicle activity is high. I would therefore request that the Council arrange an inspection of the full row of street trees, with particular attention to: * The extent of basal growth and its impact on footway width and pedestrian safety * The level of honeydew deposition and whether any management measures (such as pruning or maintenance adjustments) could reduce its severity * Whether the current maintenance regime remains appropriate given the location, traffic conditions, and nearby nursery I fully understand and support the importance of urban trees and am not requesting removal solely on the basis of seasonal nuisance.I believe the combined impact across the whole row of trees justifies a coordinated inspection and review.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Moorfield Road, Goddington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
